<<<<<<< HEAD
---
title: "Converting Agewell Codesheet to long Format Sequential"
author: "Prerana Chandratre"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
# Run this file before running the 'new_long_format_nonseq.rmd' file for the final results
```

```{r setup, include=TRUE}
# Load Required Libraries and Import Data
library(openxlsx)   # for reading/writing Excel files
library(dplyr)      # for data manipulation
library(tidyr)      # for reshaping data
library(stringr)    # for string pattern matching

# Define file paths
file_path <- "data/included_studies_1-13.xlsx"           # input file path
output_path <- "output/long_format_with_seq_corr.xlsx" # unused output path

# Read the Excel file starting from row 2, assuming headers start there
data <- read.xlsx(file_path, startRow = 2)

#  Function to clean and standardize column names
clean_names <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub('["\']', "", x)          # Remove quotation marks
  x <- gsub("&#10;?", "", x)         # Remove HTML newline codes
  x <- gsub("[\r\n]", "", x)         # Remove actual newline characters
  x <- trimws(x)                     # Trim leading/trailing spaces
  x <- gsub("\\s+", "", x)           # Remove inner spaces
  return(x)
}

# Apply the cleaning function to column names
names(data) <- clean_names(names(data))

```


```{r static-and-dynamic-columns}
# Define Static Columns and Filter WB Variables


# List of static columns (subject identifiers, authors, year, etc.)
keep_static <- c("0_IDF", "1_ID", "1.1_CaseID", "2_Author", "3_Year", "29_WbPoint", "10_Size")

# Regex patterns to match dynamic WB columns
wb_patterns <- c(
  "^\\d+_WBmt\\d+$",           # e.g. 12_WBmt1
  "^\\d+_WBsdt\\d+$",          # e.g. 12_WBsdt1
  "^\\d+_WBt(\\d+)t(\\d+)$",   # e.g. 12_WBt1t2
  "^\\d+_WBcort(\\d+)t(\\d+)$" # e.g. 12_WBcort1t2
)

# Collect columns that match the WB patterns
wb_cols <- unique(unlist(lapply(wb_patterns, function(p) grep(p, names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE))))

# Filter to include only time-sequential intervals (e.g., 1-2, 2-3, ... 11-12)
is_sequential <- function(name) {
  match <- str_match(name, "t(\\d+)t(\\d+)")
  #if (any(is.na(match[1, 2:3]))) return(TRUE) 
  if (is.na(match[1,2]) || is.na(match[1,3])) return(TRUE)  # Allow mt/sdt columns without time ranges
  return(as.integer(match[1,3]) - as.integer(match[1,2]) == 1)  # Ensure intervals are sequential
  #return(as.integer(match[1, 3]) - as.integer(match[1, 2]) == 1)
}
wb_cols <- wb_cols[sapply(wb_cols, is_sequential)]

# Combine static and dynamic columns
all_cols <- intersect(c(keep_static, wb_cols), names(data))

# Subset the data with selected columns
data <- data[, all_cols, drop = FALSE]

# Convert all non-static (i.e., dynamic WB) columns to numeric
data[setdiff(names(data), keep_static)] <- lapply(data[setdiff(names(data), keep_static)], as.numeric)

```

```{r Adding time intervals}
# Define Time Intervals
timepoints <- 1:11                       # From timepoint 1 to 11 (for intervals 1-2 to 11-12)
intervals <- paste0(timepoints, "-", timepoints + 1)  # Create interval labels (e.g., "1-2", "2-3", etc.)

# Prepare an empty list to store reshaped rows
long_rows <- list()

```


```{r Transform Each Row into Long Format}

# Predefine expected WBmt and WBsdt column names
all_wbmt_cols <- paste0("_WBmt", 1:12)
all_wbsdt_cols <- paste0("_WBsdt", 1:12)

# Loop over each case/row
for (i in 1:nrow(data)) {
  static_info <- data[i, keep_static]  # Extract static info for this row

  # Iterate over each time interval (1-2, 2-3, ..., 11-12)
  for (tp in timepoints) {
    interval_label <- paste0(tp, "-", tp + 1)

    # Dynamically get column names for required variables for this interval
    mean1_col       <- grep(paste0("_WBmt", tp, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
    sd1_col         <- grep(paste0("_WBsdt", tp, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
    mean2_col       <- grep(paste0("_WBmt", tp + 1, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
    sd2_col         <- grep(paste0("_WBsdt", tp + 1, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
    timelag_col     <- grep(paste0("_WBt", tp, "t", tp + 1, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
    correlation_col <- grep(paste0("_WBcort", tp, "t", tp + 1, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)

    # Skip if any key column is missing
    if (length(mean1_col) != 1 || length(sd1_col) != 1 ||
        length(mean2_col) != 1 || length(sd2_col) != 1 ||
        length(timelag_col) != 1 || length(correlation_col) != 1) {
      next
    }

    # Create a new row for long format
    row <- static_info
    row$interval <- interval_label
    row[[mean1_col]] <- data[[mean1_col]][i]
    row[[sd1_col]] <- data[[sd1_col]][i]
    row[[mean2_col]] <- data[[mean2_col]][i]
    row[[sd2_col]] <- data[[sd2_col]][i]
    row[[timelag_col]] <- data[[timelag_col]][i]
    row[[correlation_col]] <- data[[correlation_col]][i]

    # Append the constructed row to long_rows list
    long_rows[[length(long_rows) + 1]] <- row
  }

  # Ensure all mean/sd columns are present in each row even if NA
  for (col in c(all_wbmt_cols, all_wbsdt_cols)) {
    if (!(col %in% names(row))) {
      row[[col]] <- NA
    }
  }
}


```

```{r Merging Output}
# Combine Long Format Data
long_format <- bind_rows(long_rows)     # Combine all reshaped rows into a single data frame
long_format[long_format == ""] <- NA    # Replace empty strings with NA

```

```{r Timepoints processing}
# Add Timepoints and Rename Original Column

long_format <- long_format %>%
  mutate(timepoints = as.numeric(`29_WbPoint`)) %>%  # Convert timepoints to numeric
  select(-`29_WbPoint`)                              # Rename original WB point column


```


```{r Filter Rows Based on Timepoints}

# Add interval start (e.g., for "1-2", interval_start = 1)
long_format$interval_start <- as.numeric(sub("-.*", "", long_format$interval))

# Retain only rows where interval start < max timepoints
long_format <- long_format %>%
  filter(is.na(timepoints) | interval_start < timepoints) %>%
  select(-interval_start)  # Drop temporary column

```


```{r Validation for accurate Output}
# Sanity Check on Key Columns
cols_to_check <- c("0_IDF", "1_ID", "2_Author", "3_Year", "10_Size")

# Check if these columns exist in the data and print their types
for (col in cols_to_check) {
  if (col %in% names(long_format)) {
    cat(col, ":", class(long_format[[col]]), "\n")
  } else {
    cat(col, ": NOT FOUND in long_format\n")
  }
}

```

```{r Reorder columns manually from Data }

# Reorder columns manually by specifying the desired column sequence
long_format <- long_format %>%
  select(
      '0_IDF',
      '1_ID',
      '1.1_CaseID',
      '2_Author',
      '3_Year',
      'interval',
      'timepoints',
      '10_Size',
      
      '38_WBmt1', '39_WBsdt1', '40_WBmt2', '41_WBsdt2', '45_WBt1t2', '46_WBcort1t2',
      '51_WBmt3', '52_WBsdt3', '48_WBt2t3', '49_WBcort2t3',
      '53_WBmt4', '54_WBsdt4', '58_WBt3t4', '59_WBcort3t4',
      '64_WBmt5', '65_WBsdt5', '61_WBt4t5', '62_WBcort4t5',
      '66_WBmt6', '67_WBsdt6', '71_WBt5t6', '72_WBcort5t6',
      '77_WBmt7', '78_WBsdt7', '74_WBt6t7', '75_WBcort6t7',
      '79_WBmt8', '80_WBsdt8', '84_WBt7t8', '85_WBcort7t8',
      '90_WBmt9', '91_WBsdt9', '87_WBt8t9', '88_WBcort8t9',
      '92_WBmt10', '93_WBsdt10', '97_WBt9t10', '98_WBcort9t10',
      '103_WBmt11', '104_WBsdt11', '100_WBt10t11', '101_WBcort10t11',
      '105_WBmt12', '106_WBsdt12', '110_WBt11t12', '111_WBcort11t12',
    )

```


```{r merge mean and SD as i and j for each Id}

# Extract and Merge Interval Columns

# Define relevant columns
static_cols <- c("1_ID", "1.1_CaseID", "2_Author", "3_Year", "interval", "timepoints", "10_Size")

# Create list to store output rows
merged_rows <- list()

# Loop through rows in long_format
for (i in 1:nrow(long_format)) {
  row <- long_format[i, ]

  # Extract interval and parse i-j
  interval <- row$interval
  split <- str_split(interval, "-")[[1]]
  i_tp <- as.integer(split[1])
  j_tp <- as.integer(split[2])

  # Construct column names based on interval values
  WBmi_col <- grep(paste0("_WBmt", i_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)
  WBsdi_col <- grep(paste0("_WBsdt", i_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)
  WBmj_col <- grep(paste0("_WBmt", j_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)
  WBsdj_col <- grep(paste0("_WBsdt", j_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)
  WBcorr_col <- grep(paste0("_WBcort", i_tp, "t", j_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)
  WBt_col <- grep(paste0("_WBt", i_tp, "t", j_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)

  # Create compact row
  merged <- row[static_cols]
  merged$WBmi <- if (length(WBmi_col)) row[[WBmi_col]] else NA
  merged$WBsdi <- if (length(WBsdi_col)) row[[WBsdi_col]] else NA
  merged$WBmj <- if (length(WBmj_col)) row[[WBmj_col]] else NA
  merged$WBsdj <- if (length(WBsdj_col)) row[[WBsdj_col]] else NA
  merged$WBcorrij <- if (length(WBcorr_col)) row[[WBcorr_col]] else NA
  merged$WBtitj <- if (length(WBt_col)) row[[WBt_col]] else NA

  merged_rows[[length(merged_rows) + 1]] <- merged
}

# Combine all into final merged format
long_format_merged <- bind_rows(merged_rows)

# Preview the first few rows
head(long_format_merged)
```


```{r Saving Output}
# Write Final Output to Excel (unstyled)
write.xlsx(long_format_merged, "output/long_format_with_seq_corr.xlsx", rowNames = FALSE)
cat("Final long-format Excel written successfully with original column names intact.")

```


```{r Apply Interval-based Row Coloring and Export Output }

# Create workbook and worksheet
wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "Long Format")

# Write data to the worksheet with bold headers
writeData(wb, "Long Format", long_format, startRow = 1, headerStyle = createStyle(textDecoration = "bold"))

# Define color palette and interval labels
interval_list <- paste0(1:11, "-", 2:12)
color_palette <- c(
  "#D9EAF7", "#E8F5E9", "#FFF9C4", "#FFEBEE", "#F3E5F5", "#E0F2F1",
  "#FBE9E7", "#F1F8E9", "#EDE7F6", "#FFECB3", "#ECEFF1"
)

# Apply background color based on interval
for (i in seq_along(interval_list)) {
  intv <- interval_list[i]
  color <- color_palette[i]
  style <- createStyle(fgFill = color)

  rows_to_style <- which(long_format_merged$interval == intv) + 1  # +1 for header row
  if (length(rows_to_style) > 0) {
    addStyle(
      wb, sheet = "Long Format", style = style,
      rows = rows_to_style, cols = 1:ncol(long_format_merged),
      gridExpand = TRUE
    )
  }
}

# Save the styled workbook
saveWorkbook(wb, "output/long_format_with_seq_corr_color.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
cat("Excel file saved with unique row colors for each interval.\n")


```
=======
---
title: "Converting Agewell Codesheet to long Format Sequential"
author: "Prerana Chandratre"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
# Run this file before running the 'new_long_format_nonseq.rmd' file for the final results
```

```{r setup, include=TRUE}
# Load Required Libraries and Import Data
library(openxlsx)   # for reading/writing Excel files
library(dplyr)      # for data manipulation
library(tidyr)      # for reshaping data
library(stringr)    # for string pattern matching

# Define file paths
file_path <- "data/included_studies_1-13.xlsx"           # input file path
output_path <- "output/long_format_with_seq_corr.xlsx" # unused output path

# Read the Excel file starting from row 2, assuming headers start there
data <- read.xlsx(file_path, startRow = 2)

#  Function to clean and standardize column names
clean_names <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub('["\']', "", x)          # Remove quotation marks
  x <- gsub("&#10;?", "", x)         # Remove HTML newline codes
  x <- gsub("[\r\n]", "", x)         # Remove actual newline characters
  x <- trimws(x)                     # Trim leading/trailing spaces
  x <- gsub("\\s+", "", x)           # Remove inner spaces
  return(x)
}

# Apply the cleaning function to column names
names(data) <- clean_names(names(data))

```


```{r static-and-dynamic-columns}
# Define Static Columns and Filter WB Variables


# List of static columns (subject identifiers, authors, year, etc.)
keep_static <- c("0_IDF", "1_ID", "1.1_CaseID", "2_Author", "3_Year", "29_WbPoint", "10_Size")

# Regex patterns to match dynamic WB columns
wb_patterns <- c(
  "^\\d+_WBmt\\d+$",           # e.g. 12_WBmt1
  "^\\d+_WBsdt\\d+$",          # e.g. 12_WBsdt1
  "^\\d+_WBt(\\d+)t(\\d+)$",   # e.g. 12_WBt1t2
  "^\\d+_WBcort(\\d+)t(\\d+)$" # e.g. 12_WBcort1t2
)

# Collect columns that match the WB patterns
wb_cols <- unique(unlist(lapply(wb_patterns, function(p) grep(p, names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE))))

# Filter to include only time-sequential intervals (e.g., 1-2, 2-3, ... 11-12)
is_sequential <- function(name) {
  match <- str_match(name, "t(\\d+)t(\\d+)")
  #if (any(is.na(match[1, 2:3]))) return(TRUE) 
  if (is.na(match[1,2]) || is.na(match[1,3])) return(TRUE)  # Allow mt/sdt columns without time ranges
  return(as.integer(match[1,3]) - as.integer(match[1,2]) == 1)  # Ensure intervals are sequential
  #return(as.integer(match[1, 3]) - as.integer(match[1, 2]) == 1)
}
wb_cols <- wb_cols[sapply(wb_cols, is_sequential)]

# Combine static and dynamic columns
all_cols <- intersect(c(keep_static, wb_cols), names(data))

# Subset the data with selected columns
data <- data[, all_cols, drop = FALSE]

# Convert all non-static (i.e., dynamic WB) columns to numeric
data[setdiff(names(data), keep_static)] <- lapply(data[setdiff(names(data), keep_static)], as.numeric)

```

```{r Adding time intervals}
# Define Time Intervals
timepoints <- 1:11                       # From timepoint 1 to 11 (for intervals 1-2 to 11-12)
intervals <- paste0(timepoints, "-", timepoints + 1)  # Create interval labels (e.g., "1-2", "2-3", etc.)

# Prepare an empty list to store reshaped rows
long_rows <- list()

```


```{r Transform Each Row into Long Format}

# Predefine expected WBmt and WBsdt column names
all_wbmt_cols <- paste0("_WBmt", 1:12)
all_wbsdt_cols <- paste0("_WBsdt", 1:12)

# Loop over each case/row
for (i in 1:nrow(data)) {
  static_info <- data[i, keep_static]  # Extract static info for this row

  # Iterate over each time interval (1-2, 2-3, ..., 11-12)
  for (tp in timepoints) {
    interval_label <- paste0(tp, "-", tp + 1)

    # Dynamically get column names for required variables for this interval
    mean1_col       <- grep(paste0("_WBmt", tp, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
    sd1_col         <- grep(paste0("_WBsdt", tp, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
    mean2_col       <- grep(paste0("_WBmt", tp + 1, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
    sd2_col         <- grep(paste0("_WBsdt", tp + 1, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
    timelag_col     <- grep(paste0("_WBt", tp, "t", tp + 1, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
    correlation_col <- grep(paste0("_WBcort", tp, "t", tp + 1, "$"), names(data), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)

    # Skip if any key column is missing
    if (length(mean1_col) != 1 || length(sd1_col) != 1 ||
        length(mean2_col) != 1 || length(sd2_col) != 1 ||
        length(timelag_col) != 1 || length(correlation_col) != 1) {
      next
    }

    # Create a new row for long format
    row <- static_info
    row$interval <- interval_label
    row[[mean1_col]] <- data[[mean1_col]][i]
    row[[sd1_col]] <- data[[sd1_col]][i]
    row[[mean2_col]] <- data[[mean2_col]][i]
    row[[sd2_col]] <- data[[sd2_col]][i]
    row[[timelag_col]] <- data[[timelag_col]][i]
    row[[correlation_col]] <- data[[correlation_col]][i]

    # Append the constructed row to long_rows list
    long_rows[[length(long_rows) + 1]] <- row
  }

  # Ensure all mean/sd columns are present in each row even if NA
  for (col in c(all_wbmt_cols, all_wbsdt_cols)) {
    if (!(col %in% names(row))) {
      row[[col]] <- NA
    }
  }
}


```

```{r Merging Output}
# Combine Long Format Data
long_format <- bind_rows(long_rows)     # Combine all reshaped rows into a single data frame
long_format[long_format == ""] <- NA    # Replace empty strings with NA

```

```{r Timepoints processing}
# Add Timepoints and Rename Original Column

long_format <- long_format %>%
  mutate(timepoints = as.numeric(`29_WbPoint`)) %>%  # Convert timepoints to numeric
  select(-`29_WbPoint`)                              # Rename original WB point column


```


```{r Filter Rows Based on Timepoints}

# Add interval start (e.g., for "1-2", interval_start = 1)
long_format$interval_start <- as.numeric(sub("-.*", "", long_format$interval))

# Retain only rows where interval start < max timepoints
long_format <- long_format %>%
  filter(is.na(timepoints) | interval_start < timepoints) %>%
  select(-interval_start)  # Drop temporary column

```


```{r Validation for accurate Output}
# Sanity Check on Key Columns
cols_to_check <- c("0_IDF", "1_ID", "2_Author", "3_Year", "10_Size")

# Check if these columns exist in the data and print their types
for (col in cols_to_check) {
  if (col %in% names(long_format)) {
    cat(col, ":", class(long_format[[col]]), "\n")
  } else {
    cat(col, ": NOT FOUND in long_format\n")
  }
}

```

```{r Reorder columns manually from Data }

# Reorder columns manually by specifying the desired column sequence
long_format <- long_format %>%
  select(
      '0_IDF',
      '1_ID',
      '1.1_CaseID',
      '2_Author',
      '3_Year',
      'interval',
      'timepoints',
      '10_Size',
      
      '38_WBmt1', '39_WBsdt1', '40_WBmt2', '41_WBsdt2', '45_WBt1t2', '46_WBcort1t2',
      '51_WBmt3', '52_WBsdt3', '48_WBt2t3', '49_WBcort2t3',
      '53_WBmt4', '54_WBsdt4', '58_WBt3t4', '59_WBcort3t4',
      '64_WBmt5', '65_WBsdt5', '61_WBt4t5', '62_WBcort4t5',
      '66_WBmt6', '67_WBsdt6', '71_WBt5t6', '72_WBcort5t6',
      '77_WBmt7', '78_WBsdt7', '74_WBt6t7', '75_WBcort6t7',
      '79_WBmt8', '80_WBsdt8', '84_WBt7t8', '85_WBcort7t8',
      '90_WBmt9', '91_WBsdt9', '87_WBt8t9', '88_WBcort8t9',
      '92_WBmt10', '93_WBsdt10', '97_WBt9t10', '98_WBcort9t10',
      '103_WBmt11', '104_WBsdt11', '100_WBt10t11', '101_WBcort10t11',
      '105_WBmt12', '106_WBsdt12', '110_WBt11t12', '111_WBcort11t12',
    )

```


```{r merge mean and SD as i and j for each Id}

# Extract and Merge Interval Columns

# Define relevant columns
static_cols <- c("1_ID", "1.1_CaseID", "2_Author", "3_Year", "interval", "timepoints", "10_Size")

# Create list to store output rows
merged_rows <- list()

# Loop through rows in long_format
for (i in 1:nrow(long_format)) {
  row <- long_format[i, ]

  # Extract interval and parse i-j
  interval <- row$interval
  split <- str_split(interval, "-")[[1]]
  i_tp <- as.integer(split[1])
  j_tp <- as.integer(split[2])

  # Construct column names based on interval values
  WBmi_col <- grep(paste0("_WBmt", i_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)
  WBsdi_col <- grep(paste0("_WBsdt", i_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)
  WBmj_col <- grep(paste0("_WBmt", j_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)
  WBsdj_col <- grep(paste0("_WBsdt", j_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)
  WBcorr_col <- grep(paste0("_WBcort", i_tp, "t", j_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)
  WBt_col <- grep(paste0("_WBt", i_tp, "t", j_tp, "$"), names(row), value = TRUE)

  # Create compact row
  merged <- row[static_cols]
  merged$WBmi <- if (length(WBmi_col)) row[[WBmi_col]] else NA
  merged$WBsdi <- if (length(WBsdi_col)) row[[WBsdi_col]] else NA
  merged$WBmj <- if (length(WBmj_col)) row[[WBmj_col]] else NA
  merged$WBsdj <- if (length(WBsdj_col)) row[[WBsdj_col]] else NA
  merged$WBcorrij <- if (length(WBcorr_col)) row[[WBcorr_col]] else NA
  merged$WBtitj <- if (length(WBt_col)) row[[WBt_col]] else NA

  merged_rows[[length(merged_rows) + 1]] <- merged
}

# Combine all into final merged format
long_format_merged <- bind_rows(merged_rows)

# Preview the first few rows
head(long_format_merged)
```


```{r Saving Output}
# Write Final Output to Excel (unstyled)
write.xlsx(long_format_merged, "output/long_format_with_seq_corr.xlsx", rowNames = FALSE)
cat("Final long-format Excel written successfully with original column names intact.")

```


```{r Apply Interval-based Row Coloring and Export Output }

# Create workbook and worksheet
wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "Long Format")

# Write data to the worksheet with bold headers
writeData(wb, "Long Format", long_format, startRow = 1, headerStyle = createStyle(textDecoration = "bold"))

# Define color palette and interval labels
interval_list <- paste0(1:11, "-", 2:12)
color_palette <- c(
  "#D9EAF7", "#E8F5E9", "#FFF9C4", "#FFEBEE", "#F3E5F5", "#E0F2F1",
  "#FBE9E7", "#F1F8E9", "#EDE7F6", "#FFECB3", "#ECEFF1"
)

# Apply background color based on interval
for (i in seq_along(interval_list)) {
  intv <- interval_list[i]
  color <- color_palette[i]
  style <- createStyle(fgFill = color)

  rows_to_style <- which(long_format_merged$interval == intv) + 1  # +1 for header row
  if (length(rows_to_style) > 0) {
    addStyle(
      wb, sheet = "Long Format", style = style,
      rows = rows_to_style, cols = 1:ncol(long_format_merged),
      gridExpand = TRUE
    )
  }
}

# Save the styled workbook
saveWorkbook(wb, "output/long_format_with_seq_corr_color.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)
cat("Excel file saved with unique row colors for each interval.\n")


```
>>>>>>> fb0254f3d5c6721e966e7c02d1c642553523eed8
